channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Caja Rural de Navarra asienta las bases de su digitalización con un equilibrio perfecto entre lo digital y lo humano
item.description: El sector financiero lleva años inmerso en un proceso de transformación digital con el objetivo de adaptarse a las necesidades de clientes cada vez más exigentes.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Top data storage certifications to sharpen your skills
item.description: Enterprise data storage skills are in demand, and that means storage certifications can be more valuable to organizations looking for people with those qualifications.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Judge denies Elon Musk's request to block OpenAI for-profit conversion but welcomes trial
item.description: A federal judge has denied Elon Musk’s request for a court order blocking OpenAI from converting itself to a for-profit company.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien And Barbara Ortutay, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Elon Musk’s bumbling X posts are inadvertently teaching everyone how government works
item.description: If what you don’t know can’t hurt you, Elon Musk may be in luck. With a series of candid posts on X, the White House’s resident broligarch has lately been divulging which aspects of civics and data science he appears to know little about.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Reddit co-founder Alexis Ohanian joins bid to acquire TikTok
item.description: Reddit co-founder Alexis Ohanian has joined billionaire Frank McCourt’s bid to acquire TikTok as a strategic adviser.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: This is the smallest AMD PC I've ever seen: mysterious manufacturer uses Ryzen 3 APU with surprising results
item.description: The GenMachine Zhi is an obscure mini PC, but with an AMD Ryzen 3 5425U APU and tiny compact design, it packs a mighty punch.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Cybercrime crew stole then resold hundreds of tickets to Swift concerts, prosecutors say
item.description: A cybercrime crew stole then resold more than 900 digital tickets to Taylor Swift concerts and other pricey events.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: SolarWinds buys Squadcast to speed incident response
item.description: Observability provider SolarWinds announced this week it had signed an agreement to acquire San Francisco-based Squadcast and its incident response technology for an undisclosed amount.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Minnesota considers blocking 'nudify' apps that use AI to make explicit images without consent
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Molly Kelley was stunned to discover in June that someone she knew had used widely available “nudification” technology to create highly realistic and sexually explicit videos and images of her, using family photos that were posted on social media.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Opera adds ‘Browser Operator,’ an AI agent, to its browser
item.description: Opera has announced that its browser is now equipped with Browser Operator, a built-in AI agent that can help users with a variety of tasks. For example, if an Opera user wants help buying a large pack of socks in a certain color or booking a flight, the user can ask Browser Operator can do it.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Used Tesla prices depreciated more than any other automaker in 2024
item.description: The price of used Tesla’s Model 3 and Model Y vehicles depreciated more than any other cars in 2024, according to a Fast Company analysis of CarGurus data.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Thompson, Fast Company

item.title: AirPods dying? Professor discovers microenvironment that may be responsible
item.description: UT-Austin associate professor Yijin Lu said a similar microenvironment exists in all sorts of electronics. This includes places like your phone and electric vehicles.
item.dc:creator: Eric Henrikson, NewsNation

item.title: TikTok’s ‘airport theory’ dares you to arrive just 15 minutes before your flight
item.description: When it comes to airports, travelers tend to fall into two camps. There are the anxious types who show up four hours early, with plenty of time to leisurely peruse duty-free and enjoy the airport lounge.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: The world’s thinnest phone was just revealed, but a new iPhone 17 Air leak suggests it could be even slimmer
item.description: Tecno has revealed its new Spark Slim smartphone at MWC 2025, and at 5.75mm thin it’s got a solid claim to the title of the world’s thinnest slab phone – but a new iPhone 17 Air leak suggests it could be even thinner.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Casey Anthony, mom acquitted in toddler's death, joins TikTok
item.description: Casey Anthony, a Florida mother who was acquitted of murder charges in the death of her 2-year-old daughter Caylee, has joined TikTok to brand herself as a "legal advocate.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, NewsNation

item.title: 5 questions about Trump’s crypto reserve plan
item.description: President Trump offered new details about his plan to create a strategic crypto reserve over the weekend, prompting the cryptocurrency market to rally after a few sluggish weeks.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: DeepSeek
item.description: DeepSeek, founded in 2023 by Liang Wenfeng, a Chinese entrepreneur, engineer and former hedge fund manager, is generating a lot of buzz — and for good reason. Here are five things that make it stand out (as well as a listing of the latest news and analysis about DeepSeek).
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Georgia lawmakers push to ban automated speed cameras near schools
item.description: Some Georgia lawmakers are trying to get rid of automated cameras that enforce school zone speed limits, saying the cameras are more about making money than the safety of children.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Amy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: MrBeast lost ‘tens of millions’ on his Amazon show. That’s a sign for other creators
item.description: Hollywood is waking up to the power of influencers. That starts with MrBeast. Amazon’s Beast Games cost hundreds of millions to produce. Some of that came from the streamer, but much of it came from the pockets of MrBeast, whose real name is Jimmy Donaldson.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Trump’s crypto reserve is a payoff for loyalists
item.description: President Donald Trump has found a new way to reward his supporters. On Sunday, Trump announced in a Truth Social post that he will issue an executive order creating a “Crypto Strategic Reserve” that will include Bitcoin, Ethereum, and three other cryptocurrencies. “I will make sure the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Scott Nover, Fast Company

item.title: TSMC will invest $100B in US chip manufacturing
item.description: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) plans to invest $100 billion to build chip manufacturing plants in the U.S., President Trump announced Monday. The multibillion-dollar investment, first reported by The Wall Street Journal, will stretch out over the next four years.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: UK data protection watchdog investigating how TikTok uses children's personal data
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The U.K.'s data protection watchdog said Monday that it's investigating how TikTok uses the personal information of teenagers to deliver content recommendations to them when they use the social media platform.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIA HUI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Nvidia: Latest news and insights
item.description: With its legacy of innovation in GPU technology, Nvidia has become a dominant force in the AI market. Nvidia’s partners read like a technology who’s who list – e.g.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: AI and Data Exchange 2025: IDC’s Max Claps on agencies driving AI into mission areas
item.description: Federal use cases typically fall into one of five “swim lanes,” with most impacting customer or citizen services, said the IDC Insight's federal researcher. The post AI and Data Exchange 2025: IDC’s Max Claps on agencies driving AI into mission areas first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Singapore arrests three in AI chip fraud case amid US crackdown on exports
item.description: Singapore authorities have charged three men in connection with an alleged fraud scheme involving the illicit procurement of Nvidia’s high-end AI chips. The men, two Singaporeans and one Chinese national, are accused of misrepresenting end-user details to acquire the advanced hardware.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: One Tech Tip: Getting a lot of unwanted phone calls? Here are ways to stop them
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Unwanted phone calls are out of control. Whether it's a robocall trying to sell you something or spam calls from scammers trying to rip you off, it's enough to make you want to stop answering your phone.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Critical infrastructure: The rising ransomware victim
item.description: Ferhat Dikbiyik, the chief research and intelligence officer at Black Kite, explains how critical infrastructure providers can better protect their systems. The post Critical infrastructure: The rising ransomware victim first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network
